TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: ?aw anga sun, sinsi.
'aw anga sun, sinsi.
S.TRANSL: Greetings to you, teacher.

S.FORM: ka vaivaik anga sun imaza i Hiitu ki sun a nakemuda ta su valisakedan tjanuamen, ɫa?ua ?i tja ?udain, masa tu matsatsadjatsadja itjen angata.
ka vaivaik anga sun imaza i Hiitu ki sun a nakemuda ta su valisakedan tjanuamen, lja'ua 'i tja 'udain, masa tu macacadjacadja itjen angata.
S.TRANSL: When you have left (us) here in Pingtung, how will you be in your concern for us? But what can be done, as we are so far apart.

S.FORM: ka vaivaik sun itua kisia'an ramuɫu anga men aravats tjanusun a ti sinsi.
ka vaivaik sun itua kisia'an ramulju anga men aravac tjanusun a ti sinsi.
S.TRANSL: When you left from the railway station we were very sad for you, teacher.

S.FORM: ɫa?ua aitsu nu napasusu itjen tua tulu nimadju tua pazazekazekatj nimadju ?a imaza i Tayuang, ki tjen a nametsevutsevung anga nu tsuay anga nu tjaiviliɫ anga itua naqemati a Tsemas, ayaya men a masalusalu.

lja'ua aicu nu napasusu itjen tua tulu nimadju tua pazazekazekatj nimadju 'a imaza i Tayuang, ki tjen a namecevucevung anga nu cuay anga nu tjaivililj anga itua naqemati a Cemas, ayaya men a masalusalu.
S.TRANSL: But we were convinced that if we all fulfil his teaching and his commands while he was in Taiwan, we will meet later on in the future in the place of the creator God.

S.FORM: pai uta, sinsi, ka i vavaw sun tua varukur ki sun a nakemuda, ki sun a nasevalisakedan tjanuamen anga uta, saka ki sun a namalevaleva uta tua zemuker uta tua mare?a tja kaka i Igiris.
pai uta, sinsi, ka i vavaw sun tua varukur ki sun a nakemuda, ki sun a nasevalisakedan tjanuamen anga uta, saka ki sun a namalevaleva uta tua zemuker uta tua mare'a tja kaka i Igiris.
S.TRANSL: And also, teacher, while you are on the ship how will you be in your concern for us too? And you will be glad too to reach our brothers and sisters in England.

S.FORM: saka tiamen anga uta, kalevanga ti sinsi a uri djemaɫun tua mare?a a ?a?a, ayaya men.
saka tiamen anga uta, kalevanga ti sinsi a uri djemaljun tua mare'a a 'a'a, ayaya men.
S.TRANSL: And we too will say: Good for the teacher, he will reach our brothers and sisters.

S.FORM: nu ?ana masa a demut, ?i men a nasaɫinga ta kidjadjalan tjanusun.
nu 'ana masa a demut, 'i men a nasaljinga ta kidjadjalan tjanusun.
S.TRANSL: If it was near we would want to go with you;

S.FORM: ɫa?ua ?i tja kudain a matsatsadjatsadja itjen a ma?eɫang.
lja'ua 'i tja kudain a macacadjacadja itjen a ma'eljang.
S.TRANSL: but what can we do as we are so far apart, as we know?

S.FORM: ɫa?ua aitsu nu tjaɫuzuanga sun tua mare?a tja kaka, ki sun a napaqeneqenetj anga tjanuamen.
lja'ua aicu nu tjaljuzuanga sun tua mare'a tja kaka, ki sun a napaqeneqenetj anga tjanuamen.
S.TRANSL: But when you get there to our brothers and sisters, you will remember us;

S.FORM: nu inulinuli sun ki su inulinulian amen uta.
nu inulinuli sun ki su inulinulian amen uta.
S.TRANSL: and when you pray you will pray for us too.

S.FORM: saka tiamen uta uɫa zemangazangal ti sinsi, uɫa padjalidjalim ti sinsi tu sipangtjez anga tjanuitjen nu tjaiviliɫ, ?i men a ayaya uta nu inuli amen.
saka tiamen uta ulja zemangazangal ti sinsi, ulja padjalidjalim ti sinsi tu sipangtjez anga tjanuitjen nu tjaivililj, 'i men a ayaya uta nu inuli amen.
S.TRANSL: And when we pray we will say: May the teacher persevere, may the teacher be keen to come back to us later.

S.FORM: sa?a aitsu, sinsi, a mare?a tja matjaɫaɫak i zua mare?a tja kaka ?i navenala ?emuda nu djemaɫun sun.
sa'a aicu, sinsi, a mare'a tja matjaljaljak i zua mare'a tja kaka 'i navenala 'emuda nu djemaljun sun.
S.TRANSL: And now, teacher, the parents there and our brothers and sisters, I hope they will be well when you reach them.

S.FORM: aitsu nu mapaqeneqenetj ?i sun a ventsivetsik anga tjanuamen sa su pasemalamalavi anga men tu ki nakemuda su zinukeran, tu ki sun a nasuɫivatj ?emuda i vavaw tua varukur.
aicu nu mapaqeneqenetj 'i sun a vencivecik anga tjanuamen sa su pasemalamalavi anga men tu ki nakemuda su zinukeran, tu ki sun a nasuljivatj 'emuda i vavaw tua varukur.
S.TRANSL: And when you think of us you will write to us and tell us about your arrival, whether you were all right on the ship.

S.FORM: saka aitsu ?i men a ?aɫavaɫava uta tua su vetsik uta, sinsi.
saka aicu 'i men a 'aljavaljava uta tua su vecik uta, sinsi.
S.TRANSL: And now we will await your letter, teacher.

S.FORM: nu binkiukiu anga sun uta ?i sun a pasemalamalaw uta tua uri su sipangtjezan anga tjanuamen nu tjaiviliɫ anga.
nu binkiukiu anga sun uta 'i sun a pasemalamalaw uta tua uri su sipangtjezan anga tjanuamen nu tjaivililj anga.
S.TRANSL: When you have begun studying too you will tell us when you are going to come back to us in the future.

S.FORM: sa?a tia?en uta, sinsi, tua naramaɫeng a?en aravats aitsu nu mangtjez anga sun nu tjaiviliɫ, nu lemaqets ?i nane?anga.
sa'a tia'en uta, sinsi, tua naramaljeng a'en aravac aicu nu mangtjez anga sun nu tjaivililj, nu lemaqec 'i nane'anga.
S.TRANSL: And I too, teacher, as I am very old, when you come back in the future perhaps I will not be here (?).

S.FORM: ɫa?ua nu pasusu anga ?en, nu namakaya ken a kitjaɫupatsay tua ?u ?inaimazan i ?atsauan, nu ayain a tjaisangas anga tjanusun a sema tinkuku.
lja'ua nu pasusu anga 'en, nu namakaya ken a kitjaljupacay tua 'u 'inaimazan i 'acauan, nu ayain a tjaisangas anga tjanusun a sema tinkuku.
S.TRANSL: But if I am faithful, if I can endure my time on earth, perhaps (?) I will go to heaven before you.

S.FORM: ɫa?ua aitsu nu ini anan a pasusu a?en, ?asinu ?i nazemuker tua ?ama i ?alevelevan.
lja'ua aicu nu ini anan a pasusu a'en, 'asinu 'i nazemuker tua 'ama i 'alevelevan.
S.TRANSL: But if I am still not faithful, how can I reach our Father in heaven?

S.FORM: ɫa?ua aitsu nu sekulukulung amen i maza, ?i sun a napazekatj anga tjanuamen.
lja'ua aicu nu sekulukulung amen i maza, 'i sun a napazekatj anga tjanuamen.
S.TRANSL: But if we here are stupid, you will forgive us.

S.FORM: ?aw anga sun, sinsi.
'aw anga sun, sinsi.
S.TRANSL: Greetings, teacher.

S.FORM: djavadjavay a su kipaula tjanuamen ?a imaza anan sun i Tayuang.
djavadjavay a su kipaula tjanuamen 'a imaza anan sun i Tayuang.
S.TRANSL: Thank you for your trouble on our behalf while you were still here in Taiwan.

S.FORM: amin.
amin.
S.TRANSL: The end.
